title: oxyceros horridus
intro: Oxyceros horridus, also known as the Wild Jasmine or Indian Wild Jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Oxyceros horridus lie in its fragrant flowers, which are used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it an effective remedy for digestive problems and skin infections. Oxyceros horridus is typically prepared as a herbal tea or oil, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
